q: I like whoever says so.
o: 1. I like anyone who says so.
2. I like no matter who says so.

q: Whoever may say so, it is not true.
o: 1. Anyone who may say so, it is not true.
2. No matter who may say so, it is not true.

q: Whatever my mom cooks is delicious.
o: 1. Anything that my mom cooks is delicious.
2. No matther what my mom cooks is delicious.

q: Whoever comes, don't open the door.
o: 1. Anyone who comes, don't open the door.
2. No matter who comes, don't open the door.

q: Whoever comes first will get money.
o: 1. Anyone who comes first will get money.
2. No matter who comes first will get money.

q: I'll give a present to whoever studies English.
o: 1. I'll give a present to anyone who studies Englsih.
2. I'll give a present to no matter who studies English.

q: Whatever happens, he won't come back home.
o: 1. Anything that happens, he won't come back home.
2. No matter what happens, he won't come back home.

q: Whatever you say, I believe John is honest.
o: 1. Anything that you say, I believe John is honest.
2. No matter what you say, I believe John is honest.

q: I'd like to work with whoever is very hard-working.
o: 1. I'd like to work with anyone who is very hard-working.
2. I'd like to work with no matter who is very hard-working.

q: I'll go fishing with whoever wants to go.
o: 1. I'll go fishing with anyone who wants to go.
2. I'll go fishing with no matter who wants to go.

q: You can choose whomever you want to study with.
o: 1. You can choose anyone whom you want to study with.
2. You can choose no matter whom you want to study with.

q: Whatever my friends say, I'll study English.
o: 1. Anything that my friends say, I'll study English.
2. No matter what my friends say, I'll study English.

q: Give this book to whoever wants it in the class.
o: 1. Give this book to anyone who wants it in the class.
2. Give this book to no matter who wants it in the class.

q: I'll give you whatever you want.
o: 1. I'll give you anything that you want.
2. I'll give you no matter what you want.

q: Whatever you want, you should do your best.
o: 1. Anything that you want, you should do your best.
2. No matter what you want, you should do your best.

q: Whoever buys this car should have it repaired before driving it.
o: 1. Anyone who buys this car should have it repaired before driving it.
2. No matter who buys this car should have it repaired before driving it.

q: Whoever wins the game will receive this prize.
o: 1. Anyone who wins the game will receive this prize.
2. No matter who wins the game will receive this prize.

q: Whoever you are, be quiet!
o: 1. Anyone who you are, be quiet!
2. No matter who you are, be quiet!

q: You can take whatever you want.
o: 1. You can take anything that you want.
2. You can take no matter what you want.

q: Whatever they say, never mind.
o: 1. Anything what they say, never mind.
2. No matter what they say, never mind.
